artPrompt: A dramatic moment as a cluster of straining townsfolk haul on knotted ropes to wrench an entrenched municipal fixture clean out of the town green, dark tangled roots ripping free of the soil in a shower of dirt clods, the object tilting against a bright sky. Onlookers cheer, picket fences and casserole tables behind. Golden afternoon light, leafy green and ivory, gleeful uprooting energy composed wide in a 4:3 landscape frame, rendered as a vibrant gouache storybook illustration — saturated matte color, simplified flat-ish shapes, soft painterly edges, decorative.
name: Uproot the Incumbent
text: Destroy target artifact or enchantment an opponent controls. If that player controls more permanents than you, create two 1/1 green and white Voter creature tokens. (Pull up the entrenched stuff. Roots and all.)
flavorText: The mayor called it a "municipal fixture." The recall called it "a thing we can absolutely remove with enough volunteers."
